evidence.sanitizedResult: Workflow runtime state can publish runtime trace and group records; store tests cover run success, trace sync retry/failure, generated image history hydration, and transient generated image records when artifact persistence fails.

summary: Authority scan found explicit auth boundary scripts, request-scoped workspace permission checks, artifact route workspace gates, and provider auth separation from Supabase auth.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Routes and scans distinguish Supabase Authorization from provider runtime authorization. Artifact routes require workspaceId and workspace permission. Image generation uses X-Nexus-Runtime-Authorization or server env for provider key and request-scoped workspace permission in production.

summary: Frontend state scan identified a large Nexus operations component and store as the main state ownership pressure points.

evidence.sanitizedResult: nexus-ops.tsx is 9542 lines, nexus-graph.tsx is 2345 lines, workflow-pro-surface.tsx is 1721 lines, nexus-store.ts is 4814 lines. State spans view mode, auth vault, composer, artifacts, runtime nodes, trace sync, recovery, and provider credentials.

summary: Credential boundary scan confirmed local persistence intentionally scrubs raw auth/provider secrets while runtime memory can hold Global API Vault values.

evidence.sanitizedResult: prepareAuthVaultForLocalPersistence sets globalApiKey and provider apiKey values to null; tests assert raw provider secrets are not written to local persistence. setGlobalApiKey still loads a runtime memory value and sets streamMode live while the UI session is active.

summary: The missing Global API Vault value can be a session/runtime state issue rather than absent configured provider credentials.

inference.claim: If a user expects the raw key to remain visible after reload, current persistence design will falsify that expectation by scrubbing it. For live testing, Computer Use must load the vault in the current localhost session, then provider routes can use it through runtime headers or server env fallback.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: Computer Use showing the vault already contains an unlocked/locked key after a fresh reload, or tests showing raw key persists in local storage.

summary: Real provider verify passed through localhost route using configured OpenAI-compatible credentials without mock or dry-run.

evidence.sanitizedResult: HTTP 200, ok=true, verified=true, provider=openai-compatible, model=gpt-4o-mini, checkedAt=2026-06-04T19:20:30.756Z, error=null.

summary: The configured provider key works for a real OpenAI-compatible provider call; explicit providerId must follow the local registry mapping for the selected model.

inference.claim: Provider verification should omit providerId or use the registry provider id for gpt-4o-mini. The credential is valid for real provider calls through the localhost route.

summary: Read 00-active-checkpoint.md before starting the live account/provider/artifact matrix phase.
Started live account/provider/artifact matrix covering three new account probes, real LLM communication, real image generation, artifact/history/storage checks, and UI vault gate rechecks.

phase.primaryGoal: Verify account/workspace/provider/artifact behavior from first principles with localhost UI/Computer Use and real provider/API evidence where configured.

summary: Route-contract scan found AuthScreen sign-up/login UI, workspace session mutation idempotency, v1 agent stream Supabase actor requirement, and image/artifact workspace contracts.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Sign-up can return a live Supabase session; mutation routes require X-Idempotency-Key; v1 agent stream requires a valid Supabase actor and can use X-Nexus-Runtime-Authorization/server env for provider key; image generation can use server/runtime provider credentials and materialize generated image assets by workspaceId.

summary: First three-account API probe created sessions but failed workspace mutation due missing idempotency header, with downstream v1 stream returning 403.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Accounts A/B/C signUpOk=true and session_returned. Each workspace session request returned HTTP 400 IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_MISSING. Each subsequent v1 stream returned HTTP 403 with no token events/done. No raw passwords, access tokens, or provider keys were printed.

summary: S-P02-IDEMPOTENCY-001: account stream failures may be caused by missing mutation idempotency during workspace bootstrap rather than provider connectivity.

suspicion.claim: Adding X-Idempotency-Key to workspace/session and mutation probes may allow workspace bootstrap and change v1 stream results from 403 to token/done or a more specific error.

summary: Second three-account probe with idempotency created owner workspaces, but v1 agent stream still returned permission denied when using the originally preferred workspace IDs.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Accounts A/B/C signUpOk=true and session_returned. Workspace/session returned HTTP 200 ok=true role=owner reason=created_user_workspace for all three with idempotency headers. V1 stream returned HTTP 403 PERMISSION_DENIED for all three with zero token events. The returned workspaceId hashes differed from the originally preferred workspaceId hashes.

summary: S-P02-WORKSPACE-ID-001: v1 stream permission denial may be caused by streaming against the preferred workspaceId instead of the server-returned workspaceId.

suspicion.claim: Workspace/session may ignore or normalize the preferred workspaceId. The next stream probe must use the workspaceId returned by the session route, not the client-preferred id.

summary: Third three-account probe passed using server-returned workspace IDs: all accounts streamed real LLM responses through v1 agent stream.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Accounts A/B/C signUpOk=true, session_returned, workspace/session HTTP 200 role=owner reason=created_user_workspace, v1 agent stream HTTP 200, task id header present, tokenEvents=5 each, hasDone=true each, hasExpectedMarker=true each, hasError=false. Workspace streams used server-returned workspaceId hashes. No raw emails, passwords, access tokens, or provider keys were printed.

summary: Using the server-returned workspaceId resolves the three-account v1 stream permission denial; preferred workspace IDs are not authoritative after session bootstrap.

inference.claim: The session route can return a workspaceId different from the preferred id, and downstream account-scoped runtime calls must use the returned workspaceId to pass permission checks.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: A later session route change that guarantees preferredWorkspaceId is returned unchanged, or UI code proving it already always switches to the returned workspaceId before stream calls.

summary: Computer Use clicked START ALL after Vault LOCKED and observed a live runtime failure for multi-start Lite Runner.

sourceRefs: Google Chrome: http://localhost:3000/ Workflow canvas top toolbar START ALL
Google Chrome: Workflow runtime status chip
evidence.sanitizedResult: With Providers panel still showing Global API Vault LOCKED, START ALL was clicked in the workflow canvas. The runtime status changed from success to FAILED WORKFLOW RUNTIME LITE and displayed the message that the current Lite Runner does not yet support multiple starting input.text nodes. No provider success is claimed for START ALL; this is a live UI/runtime failure evidence item.

summary: START ALL is visible and clickable in the Workflow Pro UI, but the Lite Runner rejects the multi-start workflow shape.

contradiction.evidenceA: Workflow canvas exposes a START ALL button and the user can click it through Computer Use.
contradiction.evidenceB: Live runtime status after click says FAILED WORKFLOW RUNTIME LITE because the Lite Runner does not yet support multiple starting input.text nodes.
contradiction.currentInterpretation: The START ALL affordance is not equivalent to successful multi-start execution in the current local Workflow Pro runtime.

summary: Probe a single workflow input START after START ALL exposed the multi-start Lite Runner limitation.

nextProbe.question: Can a single visible input START run fresh after the localhost Vault is locked, and does it produce live LLM/image/runtime output?
nextProbe.blockedIf: Single input START is not clickable, runtime remains unchanged, or provider-backed nodes fail without usable status.

summary: Read 00-active-checkpoint.md before starting the second-stage scan boundary correction phase.
Started second-stage scan boundary correction to separate scan-enabling repair from formal repair work.

phase.primaryGoal: Clarify that earlier minimal code edits were made only to unblock black-box scanning and are not the formal repair phase outcome.

summary: Local diff review identified the current scan-enabling code-edit scope and separated it from unstarted formal repair work.

evidence.sanitizedResult: The current local edits include sanitized provider status exposure, UI treatment of server-configured provider readiness, workspace session routing/idempotency support, and focused tests. These edits are reclassified as scan-enabling repair-prep because they unblock provider/account/workspace scanning; the formal repair phase remains not started until the scan report is closed.

summary: Inference recorded: scan-enabling repair is allowed only as a blocker-removal layer and must not be reported as completed product repair.

inference.claim: This run is now in second-stage scan mode: minimal edits already made may be used to see the system clearly, but final reports must distinguish them from formal P0/P1/P2 repair completion.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: A later checkpoint/report claiming formal repair completion before the black-box scan closes or expanding code edits without a new scan-blocker event.

summary: Risk recorded: repair-prep began before full scan closure and must be constrained to scan blockers.

risk.blastRadius: Reports may overstate completed repair if scan-enabling edits are not labeled correctly.
risk.nextAction: Update final-report and repair-plan wording, then resume A/B/C UI scanning without broadening repair scope.

summary: Inference recorded: disposable UI account A can reach a provider-backed LLM response after the Vault gate.

inference.claim: For account A, the visible localhost UI sign-up plus Global API Vault lock plus workspace chat response is sufficient to mark the A account UI LLM probe as passed. This inference does not cover accounts B or C.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: A later account A repeat showing the same UI marker without a provider-backed request, or logs proving the chat response was produced by mock/static code rather than the configured provider.

summary: Initial Round 5 API image/artifact probe passed real image generation and generated asset retrieval but artifact creation returned 500; a minimal artifact create probe also returned 500.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Temporary Supabase user/workspace creation succeeded and real image generation returned durable supabase-storage image/png assets, but artifact create returned INTERNAL_ERROR 500 in both generated-image and minimal note variants. This evidence is failure evidence only and was not claimed as a pass.

summary: Direct Supabase insert and migration scan explained the Round 5 artifact create probe failure as provenance field/schema mismatch, not provider failure.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Direct insert returned Postgres 22P02 for a prefixed source_task_id value. Static migration scan shows artifacts.source_task_id and artifacts.source_tool_run_id are uuid columns with foreign key constraints. Inference recorded separately: ad hoc probes should omit these provenance fields unless they reference existing durable task/tool_run rows.

summary: Corrected Round 5 real provider/API image-artifact probe passed workspace, provider, storage, artifact create/list, and artifact asset download.

evidence.sanitizedResult: After omitting unproven sourceTaskId/sourceToolRunId provenance fields, the API probe passed end to end: provider=supabase-storage, durable=true, mimeType=image/png, imageBytes=1115234, assetBytes=1115234, downloadBytes=1115234, assetId=img_450c2980-a13a-4fc9-96e0-c2075f37b3c0, artifactId=4221cc89-7c1c-45fd-a3ae-78d791b96179, contentHash=4466dd8e1e3ba72d. No raw secret, bearer token, cookie, or account credential was recorded.
